text.title: GitOps — Progressive Delivery Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Promotion between rollout steps MUST be gated by metric thresholds (success rate ≥SLO, latency ≤SLO, error rate ≤SLO) over a defined window. Time-based promotions without metrics are rejected.
rule.rationale: Time-based promotions miss real failures; metric gates promote only when health is verified.

rule.statement: Metric breach MUST trigger automatic rollback to the previous stable revision. Manual rollback as the only path is rejected.
rule.rationale: Manual rollback during incidents is too slow; auto-rollback caps blast radius.

rule.statement: Canary rollouts MUST define explicit step sequence (e.g. 5%→25%→50%→100%) with per-step duration. Implicit 'gradual' rollouts are rejected.
rule.rationale: Implicit rollouts vary run-to-run; explicit steps are debuggable and tunable.

rule.statement: Canary MUST run alongside a baseline of the previous revision for direct comparison; not against historical traffic.
rule.rationale: Historical comparison drifts (load patterns differ hour-to-hour); side-by-side baseline isolates the change.

rule.statement: Rollout configuration MUST be checked into Git alongside the deployment, versioned, owned, and reviewed.
rule.rationale: Rollout config drift is rollout failure; treat it as code.

rule.statement: Skip when no traffic-split primitive OR no metric source OR static-VM infra OR sub-weekly deploys.
rule.rationale: Progressive delivery economics break below these thresholds; simpler patterns win.
